reason: Regulatory capture signal: exempts ERISA-governed plans from ACA Essential Health Benefits, creating a tiered benefit structure that protects plan sponsors (typically large employers) while shifting cost/risk exposure onto covered populations and public health systems. Classic carve-out that reads as market segmentation for positioned actors.
vectors: Regulatory capture: categorical exemption from federal health benefit standards
Entitlement erosion: EHB mandate removal reduces coverage guardrails, likely externalities to Medicaid/public systems
Asymmetric risk transfer: benefit shrinkage concentrated on plan members, not sponsors
ERISA loophole expansion: widening already-substantial ERISA preemption corridor
title: To amend the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 and the Public Health Service Act to exempt certain employer-sponsored group health plans governed by ERISA from the Affordable Care Act's Essential Health Benefits requirements, and for other purposes.

reason: This is a procedural rule bundling six substantive bills with known high-risk vectors: Defense authorization (H.R. 8800) paired with Social Security disability carve-outs (H.R. 8884), a 10-year budget resolution (fiscal bleed lever), banking law amendments (H.R. 6955 is likely regulatory capture/carve-out vehicle), and a continuing appropriation (H.R. 9770) that obscures true spending and delays transparency.
vectors: Defense authorization bundled with entitlement rider (Social Security disability reauth) — classic smokescreen pairing
10-year budget resolution (H. Con. Res. 113) establishes fiscal framework for future deficit mechanics and reconciliation runways
H.R. 6955 banking amendments — 'improvements to Federal banking laws' is regulatory capture language; likely contains safe harbors or exemptions
Continuing appropriation (H.R. 9770) defers transparency and maintains existing carve-outs without reauthorization scrutiny
Procedural rule itself bundles six bills to reduce amendment opportunity and compress legislative review window
title: Providing for consideration of the bill (H.R. 8800) to authorize appropriations for fiscal year 2027 for military activities of the Department of Defense, for military construction, and for defense activities of the Department of Energy, to prescribe military personnel strengths for such fiscal year, and for other purposes; providing for consideration of the bill (H.R. 8884) to amend title II of the Social Security Act to reauthorize demonstration authority for the disability insurance program; providing for consideration of the concurrent resolution (H. Con. Res. 113) establishing the congressional budget for the United States Government for fiscal year 2027 and setting forth the appropriate budgetary levels for fiscal years 2028 through 2036; providing for consideration of the bill (H.R. 7008) to amend chapter 131 of title 5 to require certain restrictions on stocks for Members of Congress and their spouses and dependents, and for other purposes; providing for consideration of the bill (H.R. 6955) to make improvements to the Federal banking laws, and for other purposes; providing for consideration of the bill (H.R. 9770) making continuing appropriations for fiscal year 2027, and for other purposes; and for other purposes.
